Provide project context and coding guidelines that AI should follow when generating code, answering questions, or reviewing changes.


## Naming Conventions
- Use camelCase for variables and functions (e.g., `myVariable`, `calculateTotal`).
- Use PascalCase for classes and components (e.g., `UserService`, `PatientComponent`).
- Use snake_case for database fields (e.g., `first_name`, `last_name`). Ensure to include comments for clarity and maintainability.Ensure all code is properly documented with JSDoc comments where applicable./* Naming Conventions:  Use clear and descriptive names for variables and functions to enhance readability.Utilize consistent indentation and whitespace to improve code clarity.Ensure to follow the DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principle to prevent code duplication.Make sure to write unit tests for critical logic and document any external dependencies. */  /** Coding Guidelines:  Follow best practices for code structure and organization to maintain a clean and maintainable codebase.  Use meaningful commit messages when making changes to the codebase to facilitate collaboration and version control.  Regularly review and refactor code to improve performance and readability while ensuring that functionality remains intact.  Adhere to security best practices, especially when handling sensitive data or user input, to prevent vulnerabilities.  Stay updated with the latest developments in the technologies used in the project to leverage new features and improvements effectively. */  /* Naming Conventions: Ensure all naming conventions and guidelines are followed consistently throughout the codebase. This includes variable names, function names, class names, and database field names. Consistency in naming helps improve code readability and maintainability. */

## camelCase
- Use camelCase for variables and functions (e.g., `myVariable`, `calculateTotal`). This convention enhances readability and is widely adopted in JavaScript and TypeScript projects.Ensure to consistently apply camelCase throughout your variable and function declarations. This includes local variables, function parameters, and any other identifiers that are not classes or components. Consistent use of camelCase helps maintain a clean and professional codebase, making it easier for developers to understand and collaborate on the project.Ensure to follow camelCase convention for naming variables and functions. This means that the first letter of the identifier should be lowercase, and each subsequent word should start with an uppercase letter (e.g., `myVariable`, `calculateTotal`). Adhering to this convention improves code readability and consistency across the codebase.Additionally, ensure that all function names and variable declarations follow this guideline for optimal clarity and maintainability.Follow the camelCase convention for naming variables and functions, and ensure consistent indentation throughout your code. This practice enhances readability and maintainability, making it easier for developers to understand and work with the codebase. Consistent naming and formatting are crucial for collaborative projects and help prevent confusion when navigating through the code.Consistent use of camelCase enhances code readability and maintainability.
## pascalCase
- Use PascalCase for classes and components (e.g., `UserService`, `PatientComponent`). This convention is commonly used for class names and Angular components, making it easier to identify them in the codebase. Ensure to apply PascalCase consistently for all classes and components to maintain a clear and organized code structure. This includes services, components, directives, and any other class-based constructs in the project. Adhering to PascalCase for these elements helps improve code readability and makes it easier for developers to quickly identify and understand the purpose of each class or component within the codebase. Consistent use of PascalCase for classes and components enhances the overall structure and maintainability of the project.
## snake_case
- Use snake_case for database fields (e.g., `first_name`, `last_name`). This convention is commonly used in databases to improve readability and maintainability. Ensure that all database fields follow the snake_case convention to maintain consistency and clarity in the database schema. This includes table names, column names, and any other identifiers used in the database. Adhering to snake_case for database fields helps improve readability and makes it easier for developers to understand the structure of the database. Consistent use of snake_case for database fields enhances the overall organization and maintainability of the database schema. Ensure that all identifiers in the codebase follow the naming conventions outlined above to improve readability and maintainability. Consistent naming conventions across the codebase and database schema are crucial for collaborative projects and help prevent confusion when navigating through the code and database.Be sure to also apply these conventions in API responses and any data serialization methods.Ensure that all API endpoints and data fields follow the same conventions for maximum compatibility and clarity. This includes ensuring that any data sent from the backend to the frontend adheres to the camelCase convention for consistency with JavaScript and TypeScript standards, while maintaining snake_case for database interactions. Consistent naming conventions across all layers of the application enhance readability and maintainability, making it easier for developers to work with the codebase and understand the data flow between the backend and frontend.Ensure that all API endpoints and data fields follow these naming conventions for maximum compatibility and clarity, maintaining snake_case for database interactions while using camelCase for frontend data structures. This consistency across all layers of the application enhances readability and maintainability, making it easier for developers to work with the codebase and understand the data flow between the backend and frontend.

## clean code
- Ensure all code is properly documented with JSDoc comments where applicable. This practice enhances code readability and maintainability by providing clear explanations of the purpose and functionality of functions, classes, and other code constructs. Proper documentation helps other developers understand the codebase more quickly and facilitates easier collaboration. When writing JSDoc comments, be sure to include descriptions of the parameters, return values, and any exceptions that may be thrown by the function or class. This level of detail in documentation can significantly improve the usability of the codebase and reduce the learning curve for new developers joining the project. Additionally, ensure that all code is organized in a logical and consistent manner, following best practices for code structure and organization. This includes grouping related functions and classes together, using meaningful file and directory names, and maintaining a clear separation of concerns within the codebase. Adhering to these principles of clean code will help maintain a high-quality codebase that is easy to understand, maintain, and extend over time. Consistent use of clean code practices, including proper documentation and logical code organization, is essential for maintaining a high-quality codebase that is easy to understand and collaborate on. By following these guidelines, developers can ensure that the codebase remains maintainable and scalable as the project evolves.All code should be consistently formatted, with appropriate naming conventions and indentation to enhance readability.All code should be consistently formatted, with appropriate naming conventions and indentation to enhance readability. Consider best practices in style and formatting. This includes using consistent spacing, line breaks, and indentation to improve the overall readability of the code. Additionally, ensure that all code is properly documented with comments where necessary to explain complex logic or important decisions made in the code. Proper documentation can help other developers understand the code more quickly and facilitate easier collaboration on the project. Consistent formatting and thorough documentation are key components of clean code that contribute to a maintainable and understandable codebase.

## Coding Guidelines
- Follow best practices for code structure and organization to maintain a clean and maintainable codebase. This includes grouping related functions and classes together, using meaningful file and directory names, and maintaining a clear separation of concerns within the codebase. Adhering to these principles helps improve code readability and makes it easier for developers to navigate and understand the codebase. Additionally, ensure that all code is organized in a logical and consistent manner, following established design patterns and architectural principles where applicable. This can include using a modular structure for larger projects, implementing proper error handling, and ensuring that code is reusable and maintainable. By following these coding guidelines, developers can create a codebase that is easier to understand, maintain, and extend over time, ultimately leading to a more successful and sustainable project. Consistent adherence to coding guidelines is essential for maintaining a high-quality codebase that is easy to understand and collaborate on. By following best practices for code structure and organization, developers can ensure that the codebase remains maintainable and scalable as the project evolves. Additionally, regular code reviews and refactoring sessions can help ensure that the codebase continues to adhere to these guidelines and remains in good condition over time. By following these coding guidelines, developers can create a codebase that is easier to understand, maintain, and extend over time, ultimately leading to a more successful and sustainable project. Regularly reviewing and refactoring code to improve performance and readability while ensuring that functionality remains intact is also an important aspect of maintaining a high-quality codebase. Adhering to security best practices, especially when handling sensitive data or user input, is crucial to prevent vulnerabilities and protect user data. Staying updated with the latest developments in the technologies used in the project can help leverage new features and improvements effectively, ensuring that the project remains modern.

## code smells
- Avoid code smells such as duplicated code, long functions, and large classes. These issues can make the codebase harder to maintain and understand. ## Upper Snake_Case
- Use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants (e.g., `MAX_USERS`, `API_URL`). This convention is commonly used for constants to differentiate them from regular variables and functions. Ensure that all constants in the codebase follow the UPPER_SNAKE_CASE convention to maintain consistency and clarity. This includes any values that are intended to remain unchanged throughout the execution of the program, such as configuration settings, fixed values, or any other data that should not be modified. Adhering to UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants helps improve readability and makes it easier for developers to identify and understand the purpose of these values within the codebase. Consistent use of UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants enhances the overall organization and maintainability of the codebase. Ensure that all constants in the codebase follow the UPPER_SNAKE_CASE convention to maintain consistency and clarity. This includes any values that are intended to remain unchanged throughout the execution of the program, such as configuration settings, fixed values, or any other data that should not be modified. Adhering to UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constants helps improve readability and makes it easier for developers to identify and understand the purpose of these values within the codebase.Ensure that all constants in the codebase follow the UPPER_SNAKE_CASE convention to maintain consistency and clarity.const MAX_BUFFER_SIZE = 1024;  // Example of a constant following UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

## Error handling
- Implement proper error handling to ensure that the application can gracefully handle unexpected situations and provide meaningful feedback to users. This includes using try-catch blocks where appropriate, validating user input, and providing clear error messages that can help users understand what went wrong and how to fix it. Proper error handling is crucial for maintaining a positive user experience and preventing crashes or other issues that can arise from unhandled exceptions  and logging errors for debugging purposes.  Implementing global error handlers and using middleware for centralized error management. This can help ensure that all errors are handled consistently across the application and can provide a centralized location for logging and monitoring errors. Additionally, consider implementing retry logic for transient errors, such as network issues, to improve the resilience of the application. By following these guidelines for error handling, developers can create a more robust and user-friendly application that can effectively handle unexpected situations and provide meaningful feedback to users. Proper error handling is essential for maintaining a high-quality codebase and ensuring a positive user experience. Adhering to security best practices when handling errors is also crucial to prevent vulnerabilities and protect user data. This includes avoiding the exposure of sensitive information in error messages and ensuring that any user input is properly validated and sanitized to prevent injection attacks or other security issues. By following these guidelines for error handling, developers can create a more robust and secure application that can effectively handle unexpected situations and provide meaningful feedback to users while protecting their data. Regularly reviewing and testing error handling mechanisms can help ensure that they are effective and that the application can gracefully handle a wide range of potential issues. This includes testing for edge cases and ensuring that error messages are clear and helpful for users. By following these practices, developers can.

## code style
- Follow a consistent code style throughout the codebase to enhance readability and maintainability. This includes using consistent indentation, spacing, and line breaks, as well as adhering to language-specific conventions for formatting and structuring code. Consider using a linter or code formatter to enforce a consistent code style across the codebase, which can help reduce the likelihood of style-related issues and improve overall code quality. Additionally, ensure that all code is properly documented with comments where necessary to explain complex logic or important decisions made in the code. Proper documentation can help other developers understand the code more quickly and facilitate easier collaboration on the project. By following a consistent code style and providing thorough documentation, developers can create a codebase that is easier to understand, maintain, and extend over time, ultimately leading to a more successful and sustainable project. Regularly reviewing and refactoring code to improve performance and readability while ensuring that functionality remains intact is also an important aspect of maintaining a high-quality codebase. Adhering to security best practices, especially when handling sensitive data or user input, is crucial to prevent vulnerabilities and protect user data. Staying updated with the latest developments in the technologies used in the project can help leverage new features and improvements effectively, ensuring that the project remains modern and secure. By following these guidelines for code style, developers can create a codebase that is not only functional but also clean, readable, and maintainable, making it easier for developers to work with and contribute to the project over time. Consistent code style enhances readability and maintainability, making it easier for developers to understand and collaborate on the code.

## code standards
- Adhere to established coding standards and best practices for the programming languages and frameworks used in the project. This includes following language-specific conventions for naming, formatting, and structuring code, as well as adhering to best practices for security, performance, and maintainability.
